summary: A potential pre-tournament dark horse — a brave-building 4-4-2 with the best defence in South America and devastating counter pace.
style: 4-4-2 — brave build-up & rotations, inverted fullbacks with CMs popping out, wingers 1v1 to create, crosses into the box, exceptional rest-defence.
strengths: Best defence in South America — 13 cleansheets in 18 qualifiers (most), only 3 open-play goals conceded (lowest), 9× 0-0 draws
Conceded more than one goal in only 3 of their last 37 games
Devastating pace on the counter off an exceptional rest-defence

keyPlayers.name: Enner Valencia
keyPlayers.note: In the form of his international career — pace, power & goals

tactics: Build bravely with inverted fullbacks, create through wide 1v1s and crosses, but it's the elite defence and counter pace that make them a genuine dark horse.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
